steps.title: Find the growth factor for a 3-unit change
steps.content:
Rewrite $f(x)=ab^{x/n}$ as $f(x)=ak^x$, where $k=b^{1/n}$ is the multiplier for each 1-unit increase in $x$. Ratios eliminate $a$:
$$\frac{f(5)}{f(2)}=\frac{162}{6}=27$$
The input increased by $3$, so $k^3=27$ and $k=3$.

steps.title: Check it with a Desmos exponential regression
steps.content: A fast calculator route is to enter the two known points as lists and regress $y_1\sim ak^{x_1}$. Desmos finds the same per-unit multiplier, $k=3$, without separately solving for $b$ and $n$.

steps.title: Compute f(7) using the growth factor
steps.content:
From $x=5$ to $x=7$ is a 2-unit increase, so multiply by $3^2=9$:
$$f(7)=162\cdot 9=1458$$
You can also evaluate the Desmos regression model at $x=7$. The answer is 1458.
